e-commerce-application.title: Verdant Online Store
e-commerce-application.description: Developed a highly scalable and secure e-commerce platform using Angular and .NET Core (C#). Implemented JWT authentication with guards and interceptors, integrated Razorpay for payments, and designed a responsive UI optimized for both desktop and mobile using Bootstrap. Utilized Entity Framework for efficient ORM to manage database interactions.
e-commerce-application.problem: Building a secure, scalable online store with real-time payment processing and role-based admin controls — without compromising on mobile responsiveness.
e-commerce-application.approach: Architected a full-stack platform with Angular and .NET Core, using JWT guards for security, Razorpay for payments, and Entity Framework for clean data access.
e-commerce-application.impact: Delivered a production-ready e-commerce platform deployed on Azure with secure payments, responsive design across all devices, and a full admin dashboard.
keyFeatures: Developed secure JWT authentication with role-based access for a safe user experience
Integrated Razorpay for seamless payment processing and session security
Created a dynamic admin dashboard to simplify product and order management
Designed a responsive, mobile-first UI using Bootstrap, ensuring accessibility across devices
Deployed on Azure with SQL Database for scalability and reliability
Cart functionality for adding, updating, and removing items
Version control using GitHub
Database management using Entity Framework for seamless ORM operations

image-processing-application.title: Image Processing Application
image-processing-application.description: Designed and implemented a robust Java-based application for advanced image processing. The project showcases proficiency in software design patterns, modular architecture, and testing practices. It includes both a user-friendly GUI built with Swing and a flexible command-line interface for advanced scripting.
image-processing-application.problem: Creating an extensible image processing tool that supports both GUI and command-line workflows while maintaining clean, testable architecture.
image-processing-application.approach: Built a modular Java application using MVC architecture, SOLID principles, and Swing for the GUI — with comprehensive JUnit test coverage.
image-processing-application.impact: Produced a robust desktop tool supporting advanced filters, transformations, and histogram generation with both visual and scripted interfaces.
keyFeatures: Modular design using MVC architecture and SOLID principles for maintainability and scalability
Support for GUI (Swing) and CLI workflows
Advanced image processing capabilities: filtering, transformations, histogram generation, image splitting/combination
Applied TDD (Test-Driven Development) methodologies with JUnit ensuring reliability and robust error handling
Clean and reusable code leveraging OOP principles and design patterns

portfolio-website.title: Portfolio Website
portfolio-website.description: Developed a modern, interactive portfolio website using React and Tailwind CSS, optimized with Vite for efficient builds and fast development. This project showcases a deep understanding of frontend development, component-based architecture, and responsive design.
portfolio-website.problem: Designing a portfolio that reflects both design sensibility and technical depth — fast, responsive, and fun to explore.
portfolio-website.approach: Built with React and Tailwind CSS, powered by Vite for instant HMR, with component-driven architecture and smooth page transitions.
portfolio-website.impact: A performant, accessible portfolio with dynamic routing, animated sections, and continuous deployment via Vercel.
keyFeatures: Developed modular and reusable components in React for a scalable frontend architecture
Implemented dynamic routing using React Router for seamless navigation across multiple pages
Managed state and side effects efficiently with React's useState and useEffect hooks
Designed and implemented a responsive UI using Tailwind CSS, ensuring cross-device compatibility
Integrated a functional contact form with validation for collecting user inquiries
Hosted and deployed the application on Vercel with continuous integration from GitHub

food-donation.title: Food Bridge Application
food-donation.description: Designed and developed a full-stack web application to reduce food waste by connecting donors with surplus food to recipients in need. Built with Angular, Flask, and MySQL, it features a responsive user interface, secure APIs, and database management, with a focus on societal impact and technical excellence.
food-donation.problem: Millions of pounds of food go to waste daily while people go hungry. The challenge: connect surplus food donors with recipients efficiently.
food-donation.approach: Developed a full-stack app with Angular, Flask, and MySQL featuring automated expiration management, donor rewards, and a normalized database with triggers.
food-donation.impact: Created a working platform that streamlines food donation logistics with automated post management and real-time donor-recipient matching.
keyFeatures: Designed and developed a responsive frontend using Angular and Angular Material.
Built RESTful APIs with Flask for efficient data exchange and integration with MySQL.
Implemented a normalized MySQL database schema with triggers, stored procedures, and event scheduling.
Developed automated tasks to manage food post expirations and donor reward updates.

career-link.title: CareerLink Web Application
career-link.description: CareerLink is a full-stack platform designed to streamline job searches and employer outreach. It offers secure, role-based access for employers and job seekers, advanced job filtering, and AI-driven resume feedback using Google's Gemini API. Built for performance, clarity, and scale.
career-link.problem: Job seekers struggle with irrelevant listings, and employers drown in unqualified applications. Both sides need smarter tools.
career-link.approach: Built a full-stack platform with React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL — featuring AI-powered resume analysis via Google Gemini, advanced filtering, and role-based access.
career-link.impact: Delivered a platform with AI resume feedback, secure file uploads via Cloudinary, and dynamic job matching — deployed with CI/CD on Vercel.
keyFeatures: Implemented token-based authentication with HTTP-only cookies for secure user sessions
Designed Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for employers (job posting dashboard) and job seekers (application tracking)
Built advanced job filtering by location, salary range, and skills using dynamic query logic
Integrated Google AI Studio (Gemini 2.0 Flash) for AI-powered resume analysis and feedback generation
Configured secure resume upload and storage via Cloudinary for fast access and retrieval
Developed RESTful APIs using Node.js and Express, and managed relational data with PostgreSQL and Prisma ORM
Deployed the frontend on Vercel with CI/CD from GitHub for a seamless DevOps workflow

reddit-ml-sentiment-analysis.title: Social Media Sentiment Analysis
reddit-ml-sentiment-analysis.description: Analyzed over a decade of discourse evolution in the MachineLearning subreddit, tracking the rise of deep learning, sentiment trends, and community growth from 2009 to 2020. Built a comprehensive data science pipeline with NLP preprocessing, VADER sentiment analysis, SQLite database storage, and Facebook Prophet forecasting. Features an interactive Streamlit dashboard for exploring 42 tracked ML terms and visualizing trends.
reddit-ml-sentiment-analysis.problem: Understanding how the machine learning community's interests and sentiment evolved over a decade — across 300K+ Reddit posts.
reddit-ml-sentiment-analysis.approach: Built an end-to-end data science pipeline with VADER sentiment analysis, NLP preprocessing, SQLite storage, and Prophet forecasting — visualized in Streamlit.
reddit-ml-sentiment-analysis.impact: Tracked 42 ML terms revealing Deep Learning grew 890%, Transformer adoption surged 2000%, and PyTorch overtook TensorFlow by 2019.
keyFeatures: Processed 300,000+ Reddit posts and comments from 2009-2020 using advanced NLP techniques (tokenization, lemmatization, stopword removal)
Tracked 42 ML terms across 5 categories (models, architectures, frameworks, techniques, application areas) with automated term extraction
Implemented VADER sentiment analysis achieving 82% accuracy on manually labeled test set, analyzing sentiment trends over 11 years
Built modular pipeline using MVC architecture with separate modules for preprocessing, sentiment analysis, database operations, and forecasting
Designed and implemented SQLite database with normalized schema for efficient querying and data retrieval
Developed Facebook Prophet forecasting models generating 24-month predictions with confidence intervals for ML term adoption and sentiment trends
Created interactive Streamlit dashboard with 7 sections including data exploration, ML terms analysis, sentiment visualization, and forecasting charts
Identified key insights: Deep Learning grew 890%, Transformer adoption increased 2000%, PyTorch overtook TensorFlow by 2019

healthsense-iot.title: HealthSense — Real-Time IoT Health Monitoring Dashboard
healthsense-iot.description: Built a cloud-native, real-time health telemetry platform that collects and visualizes wearable sensor data (heart rate, temperature, SpO₂, steps) from thousands of simulated IoT devices. The system uses Go for backend services, React for dashboards, and AWS streaming architecture (IoT Core, Kinesis, Lambda) with Redis caching for sub-second latency. Features anomaly detection with automated SNS/Email alerts, WebSocket live updates, and comprehensive observability with CloudWatch dashboards.
healthsense-iot.problem: Monitoring health vitals from thousands of wearable devices in real-time — with sub-second latency and automated anomaly alerts.
healthsense-iot.approach: Designed a cloud-native IoT platform with Go microservices, AWS streaming (IoT Core → Kinesis → Lambda → DynamoDB), Redis caching, and WebSocket dashboards.
healthsense-iot.impact: 10,000+ concurrent devices handled at p95 latency under 800ms. Redis caching reduced DynamoDB reads by 65%. Automated alerts catch tachycardia and fever spikes.
keyFeatures: Architected and deployed a distributed IoT platform handling 10,000+ concurrent devices with p95 end-to-end latency under 800ms
Implemented MQTT ingestion via AWS IoT Core with Kinesis streaming for durable, scalable data processing at 2-second intervals
Built anomaly detection system using EWMA, z-score algorithms, and debounce logic to identify health events (tachycardia, fever spikes)
Designed Redis caching layer for hot data (last 5-10 minutes) reducing DynamoDB reads by 65% and achieving sub-second dashboard updates
Developed Go REST API and WebSocket server with JWT authentication and tenant-based data isolation for multi-tenant support
Created React dashboard with real-time Chart.js visualizations, live telemetry tiles, alert feeds, and toggle between polling vs WebSocket modes
Configured automated SNS/Email alerts with configurable thresholds and delivery tracking via CloudWatch metrics
Productionized with Terraform IaC (VPC, IoT, Kinesis, Lambda, DynamoDB, ElastiCache, SNS), Docker containerization, and CI/CD via GitHub Actions
Conducted load testing experiments comparing throughput vs latency at 1k/5k/10k device scales, polling vs WebSocket performance, and failure recovery scenarios
Implemented comprehensive observability with CloudWatch dashboards tracking Kinesis iterator age, Lambda duration/errors, DynamoDB throttles, API latency percentiles, and WebSocket connections

opsdash.title: OpsDash: Multi-Tenant Operations Dashboard
opsdash.description: Prevented cross-tenant data leaks with EF Core global query filters and JWT-based isolation. Replaced polling with SignalR WebSocket push and Redis caching for real-time operational metrics across multiple tenant environments.
opsdash.problem: Multi-tenant dashboards risk cross-tenant data leaks and stale metrics from polling-based architectures.
opsdash.approach: Implemented EF Core global query filters with JWT-based tenant isolation, replaced polling with SignalR WebSocket push, and added Redis caching.
opsdash.impact: Zero cross-tenant data leaks, 70% reduction in dashboard update latency, and significantly lower database load through intelligent caching.
keyFeatures: Implemented tenant-level data isolation using EF Core global query filters and JWT claims to prevent cross-tenant data leaks
Replaced HTTP polling with SignalR WebSocket push for real-time dashboard updates, reducing latency by 70%
Designed Redis caching layer for frequently accessed operational metrics, cutting database load significantly
Containerized the application with Docker and deployed to Azure for scalable multi-tenant hosting

canvas-sync.title: CanvasSync: Real-Time Collaborative Whiteboard
canvas-sync.description: Real-time collaborative whiteboard with HTML5 Canvas rendering, WebSocket sync, and cursor presence for multi-user editing at 60fps. Features conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) for seamless collaboration without merge conflicts.
canvas-sync.problem: Real-time collaboration tools need to handle concurrent edits without conflicts — at 60fps with zero lag.
canvas-sync.approach: Built a Canvas rendering engine with WebSocket sync via Socket.io, optimistic UI updates, and CRDT-based conflict resolution.
canvas-sync.impact: Smooth 60fps collaborative drawing with live cursor presence, real-time sync, and conflict-free concurrent editing.
keyFeatures: Built a high-performance drawing engine using HTML5 Canvas with requestAnimationFrame for smooth 60fps rendering
Implemented real-time synchronization using WebSockets and Socket.io with optimistic UI updates
Designed cursor presence system showing live collaborator positions and selections in real-time
Applied CRDT-based conflict resolution for concurrent editing without data loss

documind.title: DocuMind: AI Document Analysis
documind.description: An AI-powered document analysis platform that uses LLMs to extract insights, summarize content, and answer questions from uploaded documents. Features RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline for accurate, context-aware responses.
documind.problem: Extracting meaningful answers from large documents is tedious. Users need AI that understands context, not just keywords.
documind.approach: Built a RAG pipeline with LangChain, ChromaDB for vector search, and OpenAI API — served via FastAPI with streaming responses.
documind.impact: Context-aware document Q&A with high accuracy, real-time streaming responses, and containerized deployment for reproducibility.
keyFeatures: Built a RAG pipeline using LangChain and ChromaDB for context-aware document Q&A with high accuracy
Implemented document chunking and vector embedding for efficient semantic search across large documents
Designed a clean React frontend with real-time streaming responses from the LLM backend
Containerized the full stack with Docker for reproducible deployments

flashsale.title: Flashsale Order Processing System
flashsale.description: A high-throughput order processing system designed for flash sale events. Uses event-driven architecture with message queues to handle burst traffic, prevent overselling, and ensure order consistency under extreme load.
flashsale.problem: Flash sales generate extreme traffic spikes that crash systems, oversell inventory, and corrupt orders.
flashsale.approach: Designed an event-driven system with RabbitMQ for decoupled processing, Redis distributed locks for inventory, and idempotent payment pipelines.
flashsale.impact: Handles 10K+ concurrent requests without overselling, with exactly-once order processing validated through Artillery load tests.
keyFeatures: Designed event-driven architecture using RabbitMQ to decouple order intake from processing, handling 10K+ concurrent requests
Implemented distributed locking with Redis to prevent inventory overselling during flash sale spikes
Built idempotent order processing pipeline ensuring exactly-once semantics for payment and inventory updates
Load tested with Artillery to validate throughput and latency under simulated flash sale conditions

devflow.title: DevFlow: AI Code Review Platform
devflow.description: An AI-powered code review platform that analyzes pull requests, suggests improvements, and identifies potential bugs using LLMs. Integrates with GitHub webhooks for automated review workflows.
devflow.problem: Code reviews are time-consuming and inconsistent. Teams need automated, intelligent feedback on every pull request.
devflow.approach: Integrated GitHub webhooks with OpenAI-powered analysis for bug detection, style suggestions, and security checks — with a React review dashboard.
devflow.impact: Automated PR reviews with inline suggestions, a developer feedback loop for continuous improvement, and a clean diff-based triage interface.
keyFeatures: Integrated GitHub webhooks for automatic PR analysis and inline review comment generation
Built intelligent code analysis using OpenAI API with custom prompts for bug detection, style suggestions, and security checks
Designed a review dashboard in React showing AI suggestions alongside diff views for easy triage
Implemented feedback loop where developers can rate AI suggestions to improve future review quality
